title: doryopteris raddiana
intro: Doryopteris raddiana, also known as the Raddian Tree Fern or the Indian Tree Fern, is a plant species belonging to the family Polypodiaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate various health issues. The health benefits of Doryopteris raddiana include treating fever, diarrhea, and rheumatism, as well as acting as an antibacterial and antifungal agent. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are typically dried and used in traditional medicine to make decoctions, infusions, and poultices, which are applied topically or consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
